research.interface: Kafka Connect REST interface, worker port 8083, application/json (Confluent Platform, current docs)

official_sources.title: Kafka Connect REST Interface for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: GET /connectors with ?expand=status and ?expand=info response shapes; GET /connectors/{name}/status with connector state/worker_id and tasks id/state/worker_id/trace; POST /connectors/{name}/restart with includeTasks and onlyFailed query parameters (defaults false), response codes 200/202/204/404/409/500 and the 202 body with state RESTARTING; POST /connectors/{name}/tasks/{taskId}/restart; 409 semantics while a worker-group rebalance is in process; application/json as the only supported entity content type

official_sources.title: Monitor Kafka Connect and Connectors for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: the connector/task state model (UNASSIGNED, RUNNING, PAUSED, FAILED), the fact that Connect does not automatically restart failed tasks so states diverge, failure details surfacing in the status output, and the restart endpoints recommended for recovering failed connectors/tasks

research.language_rationale: Kafka Connect has no official C# management SDK; its documented management surface is the JSON REST interface, and C# with HttpClient/System.Text.Json is the idiomatic stack for an ops monitor embedded in the team's existing .NET tooling.

verified_facts: GET /connectors?expand=status&expand=info returns one object keyed by connector name whose values carry both the status subresource (connector state, worker_id, task states) and the info subresource (config, tasks, type); without expand parameters only a name array comes back.
GET /connectors/{name}/status reports the connector state and per-task {id, state, worker_id}; FAILED tasks include a trace field with the error stack.

POST /connectors/{name}/restart accepts includeTasks and onlyFailed boolean query parameters (both default false); includeTasks=true&onlyFailed=true restarts exactly the failed connector and task instances.

Restart responses: 202 Accepted when the restart request was durably recorded (body echoes connector status with RESTARTING instances), 204 No Content on success without a body, 404 when the connector does not exist, 409 when a rebalance makes the operation momentarily impossible, 500 when the request times out.
Connect returns HTTP 409 while a worker-group rebalance is in process because the leader may change; the operation should be retried later.
Connector and task states are UNASSIGNED, RUNNING, PAUSED and FAILED (plus RESTARTING in restart responses); Connect does not automatically restart failed tasks.
The Connect REST API supports only application/json as the request and response entity content type.
